content.problemStatement:
Alice decides to challenge Bob with a new game.

She gives Bob an integer MMM and two arrays AAA and BBB both of length NNN, such that 0‚â§Ai,Bi<M0 \leq A_i, B_i \lt M0‚â§Ai‚Äã,Bi‚Äã<M for each index iii.

Bob can perform two types operations on array BBB:


Choose an index iii (1‚â§i‚â§N(1 \leq i \leq N(1‚â§i‚â§N), and set Bi‚Üí(Bi+1)(modM)B_i\to (B_i + 1)\pmod MBi‚Äã‚Üí(Bi‚Äã+1)(modM).

That is, increase BiB_iBi‚Äã by 111, but wrap around to 000 if the value reaches MMM.

This move takes two seconds.


Choose two indices iii and jjj (1‚â§j<i‚â§N1 \leq j \lt i \leq N1‚â§j<i‚â§N), and set BiB_iBi‚Äã to BjB_jBj‚Äã.

Note that j<ij \lt ij<i, so BiB_iBi‚Äã can only be set to something before it.

This move takes one second.


Bob's task is to find the minimum time required to make array BBB equal to AAA.

Let this value equal f(A,B,M)f(A, B, M)f(A,B,M).

Bob, however, is feeling quite lazy ‚Äî and would rather not spend much time on Alice's game.

So, when Alice isn't looking, he decides to replace the array BBB entirely with another array CCC, ideally so that f(A,C,M)f(A, C, M)f(A,C,M) is as small as possible.

To ensure that Alice doesn't become suspicious at his speed though, Bob must ensure that Ai‚âÝCiA_i \neq C_iAi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=Ci‚Äã for every index iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N).

If Bob chooses such an array CCC optimally, what's the minimum possible time he needs to finish Alice's game?

Formally, find the minimum possible value of f(A,C,M)f(A, C, M)f(A,C,M) across all possible integer arrays CCC of length NNN such that:


0‚â§Ci<M0 \leq C_i \lt M0‚â§Ci‚Äã<M for each 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N; and
Ai‚âÝCiA_i \neq C_iAi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=Ci‚Äã for each 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM ‚Äî the size of the array and the bound on the array elements.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã ‚Äî the elements of the target array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line one integer: the minimum possible value of f(A,C,M)f(A, C, M)f(A,C,M) across all valid arrays.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 4‚ãÖ1054\cdot 10^54‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Bob can choose C=[3,1,2,0]C = [3, 1, 2, 0]C=[3,1,2,0], and perform the following sequence of moves:

Set C2C_2C2‚Äã equal to C1C_1C1‚Äã in 111 second. C=[3,3,2,0]C = [3, 3, 2, 0]C=[3,3,2,0] now.
Increase C1C_1C1‚Äã by 111 (modulo MMM) in 222 seconds. C=[0,3,2,0]C = [0, 3, 2, 0]C=[0,3,2,0] now.
Set C4C_4C4‚Äã equal to C2C_2C2‚Äã in 111 second. C=[0,3,2,3]C = [0, 3, 2, 3]C=[0,3,2,3] now.
Set C3C_3C3‚Äã equal to C1C_1C1‚Äã in 111 second. C=[0,3,0,3]=AC = [0, 3, 0, 3] = AC=[0,3,0,3]=A.
A total of 555 seconds were used.

It can be shown that Bob can't use less than 555 seconds, no matter which CCC he chooses (recall that Ci‚âÝAiC_i \neq A_iCi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=Ai‚Äã at any index must hold).
Test case 333: The only array Bob can choose at all is C=[1,0,1,0,1]C = [1,0,1,0,1]C=[1,0,1,0,1]. It can be verified that this can be made equal to AAA in 666 seconds.
